skill.name: popup-cro
skill.description: Create and optimize popups, modals, overlays, slide-ins, and banners for higher conversion without harming user experience.
skill.summary: Optimize popup strategy, copy, targeting, frequency, and testing for better conversion.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 11 findings, but the skill contains only Markdown guidance for popup conversion optimization. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives from marketing text; cookie/localStorage is a low privacy consideration, not executable browser storage access.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Privacy Consideration
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE as a concept but not as executable access. The skill advises remembering popup dismissals with cookies or localStorage, which is common for frequency capping but should respect consent and retention rules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line directly mentions cookie/localStorage, but the file is guidance text and contains no code that reads or writes browser storage.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines are the skill description, section headings, copy templates, and output labels; no cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found marketing and UX guidance only. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or related code was found.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited line is an example of manipulative decline copy to avoid, not a command, host inspection, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is popup decline text. No evidence of system reconnaissance, command execution, or file inspection exists at this location.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited line says popups should be easy to dismiss; it does not mention scanning, ports, hosts, requests, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is user experience guidance. No evidence of network reconnaissance or network access was found.

content.value_statement: Poorly timed popups can reduce trust and hide valuable offers. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design respectful popup strategies that improve conversion.
actual_capabilities: Recommends popup triggers based on timing, scroll depth, exit intent, clicks, sessions, and behavior.
Creates popup copy structures with headlines, subheads, calls to action, and decline options.
Advises on modal sizing, close controls, mobile behavior, accessibility, and visual hierarchy.
Defines frequency capping, targeting rules, and conflict rules for multiple popups.
Suggests A/B test ideas for placement, format, timing, messaging, and personalization.
limitations: Does not implement popup code or integrate with marketing platforms.
Does not guarantee conversion lift without traffic, analytics, and testing.
Provides general privacy guidance but does not replace legal review.
Needs business context to recommend specific offers and targeting rules.

use_cases.title: Improve Newsletter Signup Popups
use_cases.description: Create a less intrusive email capture popup with clearer value, better timing, and measurable success criteria.

use_cases.title: Reduce Ecommerce Popup Friction
use_cases.description: Plan discount, exit intent, and cart abandonment popups that avoid overlap and respect repeat visitors.

use_cases.title: Design SaaS Lead Capture Flows
use_cases.description: Use click-triggered modals and contextual offers to support demo requests, guides, and trial signups.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Popup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for an existing popup.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this popup for conversion and user experience. Goal: [goal]. Trigger: [trigger]. Copy: [copy]. Audience: [audience]. Recommend improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Popup Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a new popup from business context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a popup strategy for [business type]. The offer is [offer]. Traffic source is [source]. Include trigger, targeting, frequency, copy, and mobile notes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Multiple Popups
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a popup system for [site]. We need [popup types]. Define each popup, audience, trigger, frequency cap, and conflict rules.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced testing plan for conversion optimization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a popup A/B testing roadmap using our baseline metrics: [metrics]. Include hypotheses, variants, primary metric, guardrail metrics, and priority order.

output_examples.input: An ecommerce store wants an exit intent discount popup for first-time visitors.
output_examples.output: A recommendation with a first-purchase offer, exit intent trigger, new visitor targeting, seven-day dismissal cooldown, mobile alternative, and testable copy variants.

output_examples.input: A SaaS blog wants more demo requests without interrupting readers.
output_examples.output: A click-triggered demo modal and scroll-based guide offer, with separate audiences, neutral decline copy, accessibility notes, and measurement guidance.

output_examples.input: A publisher has high popup close rates and low newsletter conversion.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis of timing, value proposition, and frequency issues, followed by copy changes and experiments for scroll depth and page count triggers.

best_practices: Start with the popup goal, audience, trigger, and offer before writing copy.
Use frequency caps and clear dismissal controls to protect user experience.
Measure conversion rate with guardrail metrics such as close rate and mobile engagement.
anti_patterns: Showing a popup immediately before the visitor understands the page.
Using guilt-based decline copy or hiding the close option.
Running multiple popups without conflict rules or suppression logic.

faq.question: Can this skill write popup copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft headlines, subheads, calls to action, and decline text for specific popup goals.

faq.question: Does it create production popup code?
faq.answer: No. It provides strategy, copy, design notes, and testing recommendations, not implementation code.

faq.question: Can it help with mobile popups?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes mobile timing, sizing, touch target, and dismissal guidance.

faq.question: Can it plan A/B tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose hypotheses, variants, expected outcomes, and metrics for popup experiments.

faq.question: Does it cover privacy compliance?
faq.answer: It includes basic consent and preference guidance, but legal requirements need qualified review.

faq.question: When should I use another skill?
faq.answer: Use a form optimization skill for standalone forms and a page optimization skill for full-page conversion strategy.
